page: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 128 physical cores and 128 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute-intensive workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, as well as PassMark CPU, physics, and extended instruction tests. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets, yielding top-tier results in PassMark encryption tests. Memory performance is characterized by a high read-only cached small block bandwidth of 17,905.15 GB/sec and a large block uncached read bandwidth of 565.2 GB/sec.

The c7a.32xlarge demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, scoring in the top 10% for Clang compilation and Redis SET operations. It also shows high throughput for static web serving and LLM prompt processing. However, text generation speeds for larger models fall into the average tier, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance highly cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks like batch processing, scientific modeling, and high-performance web serving, though it is less suited for memory-heavy databases or GPU-dependent deep learning training.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations, with a Redis SET throughput of 261.89 million operations per second. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as scientific simulations, software compilation, high-performance web serving, and LLM prompt processing, offering strong qualitative cost efficiency for raw CPU-bound tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 128 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-performance CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.32xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 128 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core-density compute instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 system memory
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Top-tier performance in software compilation, cryptography, and database operations
Optimized for compute-bound tasks, batch processing, and static web serving
